summary: Rename the generated roll-up index file from `author.toml` to `lock.toml`

edit.description: Change the roll-up write path `into.join("author.toml")` (line ~489) to `into.join("lock.toml")` — ideally via a named `const` for the filename. Update the module and item doc comments that name the roll-up `author.toml` (lines ~28, 106, 129, 467) to `lock.toml`. Update every in-module test that asserts/reads `into.join("author.toml")` to `lock.toml`.
Update assertions referencing the generated `author.toml` to `lock.toml`.
Update assertions referencing the generated `author.toml` to `lock.toml`.
Regenerate the snapshot (`cargo insta accept`) so the imported surface names `lock.toml` in place of `author.toml`.

tests.asserts: `import` writes the roll-up to `<into>/lock.toml`; no `author.toml` remains

acceptance: `import` writes the generated roll-up state-of-record to `<into>/lock.toml`; no `author.toml` is produced; snapshots regenerated; `cargo test` / `clippy -D warnings` / `fmt --check` green.
notes: Resolves `(rollup-index-rename)`: `specs/20-surface.md` now names the generated state-of-record `lock.toml` ("The surface: a contract over its contents"; topology diagram). Filename + the docstrings/comments naming it — no behavior change, no `.temper/` topology move (the `<into>` dir is the `.temper/` contents root, so `<into>/lock.toml` is `.temper/lock.toml`). Only `src/import.rs` writes it; nothing reads it back outside import's own tests. Disjoint from the graph entries.

summary: Add an `acyclic` graph-scope check: the harness reference graph must contain no cycle

per.section: The graph scope (the model) — predicates over the relation graph

edit.description: Add `pub fn acyclic(edges, by_kind) -> Vec<Diagnostic>`: build the artifact-level directed graph by resolving each declared edge's reference field to target ids (the same arc-resolution `check` already does via `edge_targets`), then DFS for a cycle. Emit an error-severity `Diagnostic` (a `graph.acyclic` rule id) naming the cycle when the reference graph is not acyclic. Skip inadmissible edges exactly as `check` does.

Inside the existing `if let Some(layer)` graph block (~line 189-190), wire `diagnostics.extend(graph::acyclic(layer.edges(), &by_kind));` alongside `graph::admissibility` / `graph::check`. Absent `temper.toml` ⇒ no layer ⇒ no edges ⇒ adds nothing, so the floor-only path stays byte-for-byte unchanged.

Add cases: an acyclic reference graph is clean; a self-loop fires; a multi-node cycle (rule→skill→rule) fires an error; an inadmissible/dangling edge is skipped and does not mask or forge a cycle finding.

tests.asserts: a cyclic declared-reference graph fires an error-severity diagnostic; an acyclic one is clean

acceptance: `graph::acyclic` runs in `check` over the harness edge graph; a cycle among declared references fires an error-severity diagnostic; acyclic graphs pass; `cargo test` / `clippy -D warnings` / `fmt --check` green.
notes: acyclic is intrinsic to declared edges — runs always-on over `layer.edges()` like route resolution (`graph::check`), no per-role opt-in; a cycle loads nothing, so every finding is a true positive. Detects artifact-level cycles over the resolved reference arcs. The always-on vs author-declared choice is flagged here for the human. degree (opt-in, per-role) is serialized behind this — both edit graph.rs.

summary: Add a per-role `degree` clause bounding the in/out edge count of a role's matched nodes

per.section: The graph scope (the model) — predicates over the relation graph

edit.description: Add an optional degree bound to `Role` (an inclusive in/out edge-count bound, mirroring `CountBound`) and parse it off `[role.<name>]`; add the malformed-declaration error variant. Parse-only, like the other role predicates.

Add a degree check: for each role, compute the in/out degree of every artifact its `match` selects over the resolved reference arcs (reuse the arc resolution `acyclic`/`check` build) and emit an error-severity diagnostic per matched node whose degree falls outside the declared bound.
Wire the degree check into the `if let Some(layer)` graph block alongside `graph::acyclic` / `graph::check`, passing roles + edges + by_kind.
Assert a `[role.<name>]` degree bound parses into the typed `Role`; a malformed bound errors.
Assert self-registering (incoming=0, must not be pointed at) and routed (incoming≥1, must be reachable) degree bounds fire on violation and pass when the harness graph matches.

tests.asserts: an incoming=0 bound fires when a matched node is pointed at; an incoming>=1 bound fires when a matched node is unreachable; matching graphs pass

acceptance: a role declaring an in/out degree bound checks each matched artifact's edge count in the harness graph; violations fire an error-severity diagnostic; matching graphs pass; gates green.
notes: Worked example "self-registering vs routed": a role declares its pattern — incoming=0 (self-registering, must not be pointed at) or incoming>=1 (routed, must be reachable). Degree is declared on the role (set scope) but ranges over the edge graph; reuse the resolved-edge arcs graph::check builds. Exact TOML key is code's; the role-declared in/out-bound intent is spec-fixed. Blocked on ACYCLIC only for file overlap (graph.rs, main.rs, tests/graph.rs).
